Article Title: Oscars After-Party: Joe Burrow and Alix Earle spotted leaving Oscars after-party together, sending NFL fans into a frenzy
Article Text:
Image - X

Joe Burrow’s Oscars after-party exit with Alix Earle sparks wild dating speculation online



another

If there’s one thing Joe Burrow is better at than football, it’s accidentally breaking the internet during the NFL off-season. The Cincinnati Bengals quarterback is currently the talk of social media after being spotted leaving a high-profile Vanity Fair Oscars after-party in Los Angeles alongside TikTok royalty Alix Earle.While the sight of the NFL’s favorite "Golden Boy" and the internet’s most famous "Hot Mess" together sent fans into a frenzy, here is the full context of what actually went down.The buzz started when paparazzi caught Burrow leaving the party on the night of March 15, 2026. However, this wasn't exactly a romantic dinner for two. Joe was seen in the front seat of a car, while the backseat was packed with a powerhouse squad including Alix Earle, influencer Stassie Karanikolaou, and pop star Tate McRae.In the world of celebrity sightings, this was less of a "date" and more of a "very expensive Uber ride." The group had spent the night rubbing elbows with Hollywood’s elite before heading off to the next destination together.Why did this set the internet on fire? Because Burrow’s personal life is a frequent topic of debate. Just last week, he was trending for a brief interaction with Jessica Alba in Las Vegas, leading to wild (and false) dating theories.Adding to the drama is Alix Earle’s own headline-grabbing 2026. She has recently been the subject of massive speculation regarding a potential connection with Tom Brady, though Alix has consistently told her millions of followers that she is currently embracing her "single era." Seeing her in a car withlegendary quarterback was, naturally, fuel for the fire.Despite the TikTok edits and the "Shipping" comments, sources close to the group suggest there’s no secret romance here. Burrow has been famously private about his long-term relationship with Olivia Holzmacher for years, and while he’s been more active on the Hollywood social scene this spring, it seems he’s simply enjoying his time off before training camp begins.Joe Burrow is currently the undisputed king of the "off-season cameo." Whether he’s at Paris Fashion Week, a UFC fight, or leaving an Oscars party with a group of influencers, he manages to stay the coolest person in the room without saying a word.For now, Alix and Joe are just two superstars sharing a ride through the Hollywood Hills. But in the world of NFL gossip, that’s more than enough to keep everyone talking until kickoff.
Article Summary: Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow was spotted leaving an Oscars after- party with TikTok royalty Alix Earle. Burrow has been in a long-term relationship with Olivia Holzmacher for years. Alix has recently been the subject of speculation regarding a potential connection with Tom Brady.

Article Title: Singer D4Vd: Singer and streamer D4vd faces renewed scrutiny in Celeste Rivas case after Neo’s online posts
Article Text:
Getty Images

“target”

Former friend Neo’s online posts add new attention to investigation involving D4vd





Singer and streamer d4vd is once again at the center of attention after new online posts from his former friend Neo Langston added another twist to the ongoing investigation connected to Celeste Rivas Hernandez. The case has already received wide public attention since authorities began looking into the death of the 14-year-old girl. Now, Neo’s recent activity on social media has brought fresh focus to the situation and raised new questions about what may have happened.D4vd, who is 20 years old, has not been arrested or charged in the case. However, reports say he is considered ain the investigation. The situation began drawing serious concern in September 2025 when the remains of Celeste Rivas Hernandez were discovered in the trunk of a Tesla linked to the singer.The vehicle had been parked in a Hollywood tow yard. Since then, investigators have continued to review evidence and speak with people connected to the case.Recently, Neo Langston, who was once known as a close friend of D4vd and is also a streamer, shared a series of posts on Instagram. In the posts, Neo used harsh language while talking about the singer and the ongoing investigation. The messages quickly spread online and brought new attention to the case.Neo had already appeared before a California grand jury earlier during the investigation. Reports also say he was arrested last month for failing to appear before the jury at one point in the process. In the new posts, Neo suggested that he is legally protected and hinted that there may be more details about the situation than what has been publicly reported so far.The case itself has remained disturbing and complicated. According to reports, the remains of Celeste Rivas Hernandez were found in two bags inside the Tesla. The discovery was made after a strong odor was noticed coming from the vehicle at the tow yard. Authorities have not publicly confirmed how long the remains had been there, though earlier reports suggested the girl had been missing for weeks.Some reports have also mentioned a possible trip between Los Angeles and Santa Barbara in 2025 that involved people connected to the case. However, Neo’s lawyer has denied that he had any role in the crime.For now, the investigation remains ongoing. Authorities have not announced any charges against D4vd, and the legal process is still developing. As the case continues, the new posts from Neo have only increased public interest in what investigators may reveal next.
Article Summary: Former friend Neo Langston shared a series of posts on Instagram. In the posts, Neo used harsh language while talking about the singer and the ongoing investigation. The messages quickly spread online and brought new attention to the case. Authorities have not announced any charges against D4vd, and the legal process is still developing.

Article Title: Aden Holloway Arrest: Why was Alabama basketball player Aden Holloway arrested ahead of the NCAA Tournament creating major buzz around Crimson Tide program
Article Text:
Why Was Alabama Basketball Player Aden Holloway Arrested Ahead of NCAA Tournament Creating Major Buzz Around Crimson Tide Program (Image via Getty Images)

Aden Holloway arrest creates uncertainty for Alabama Crimson Tide before NCAA Tournament game

Aden Holloway is suddenly in the spotlight for the wrong reason, just days before the biggest games of the college basketball season. The Alabama Crimson Tide men’s basketball guard was arrested in Tuscaloosa after police said they found more than a pound of marijuana inside his home. According to a report by TMZ Sports, officers working with the West Alabama Narcotics Task Force searched a house on Monday. During the search, authorities said they recovered over a pound of marijuana, drug paraphernalia, and cash.Police then arrested the 21 year old junior and charged him with first degree possession of marijuana and failure to affix a tax stamp. Officials say both charges are considered felonies under Alabama law.After the arrest, Holloway was taken to the county jail. Authorities set his bond at $5,000.The news comes at a very difficult time for Alabama basketball. The team is preparing for the upcoming NCAA Division I Men’s Basketball Tournament, widely known as March Madness. So far, it is still unclear how this situation will affect Holloway’s role with the team in the tournament.Before this incident, Aden Holloway was one of the key players for Alabama this season.The young guard has been playing an important role in the team’s offense and scoring. Holloway is currently averaging 16.8 points per game for the Crimson Tide. In his most recent game, he scored 18 points during Alabama’s loss against the Ole Miss Rebels men’s basketball on Friday.Because of his scoring ability, Holloway has been a big part of Alabama’s plans heading into the NCAA Tournament. The Crimson Tide received the No. 4 seed in the tournament bracket. Their first game is scheduled against the No. 13 seed, the Hofstra Pride men’s basketball.However, Holloway’s arrest now raises serious questions about whether he will be able to play in that matchup. At the moment, officials have not confirmed what will happen next. His availability for the game will likely depend on how his legal situation develops in the coming days.For Alabama, the timing could not be worse. March Madness is the most important moment of the college basketball season, and teams rely heavily on their top players. Now the program must wait and see how the situation around Aden Holloway unfolds before the tournament begins.
Article Summary: Aden Holloway was arrested in Tuscaloosa, Alabama on Monday. Police said they found more than a pound of marijuana inside his home. The 21-year-old guard was charged with first degree possession of marijuana and failure to affix a tax stamp. Holloway is currently averaging 16.8 points per game for Alabama.

Article Title: Nhl Announcement 2028: NHL confirms World Cup of Hockey for 2028: Which cities are going to host the tournament?
Article Text:
In this Feb. 22, 1980, the U.S. ice hockey team rushes toward goalie Jim Craig after their 4-3 upset win over the Soviet Union. (AP Photo/File)

NHL announces World Cup of Hockey 2028, multiple cities to host the tournament

The National Hockey League ( NHL ) has officially announced the 2028 World Cup of Hockey, marking the return of the international best-on-best tournament after more than a decade. The tournament is scheduled for February 2028 and will feature eight national teams competing across the three cities. According to reports, the event last took place in 2016, when Canada captured the title in Toronto.Even in the other two best-on-best events held in the last two years, Canada won the 4 Nations Face-Off in 2025, before the U.S. earned revenge with Olympic gold in February.Reports suggest that the 2028 World Cup will feature eight teams; however, Russia's participation is still up in the air. In addition to this, it is reported that an original group of 25 applicants will bid to host the tournament. The NHL has confirmed the World Cup of Hockey, but which cities are going to host the tournament?The NHL has officially confirmed that the 2028 World Cup of Hockey will take place in multiple host cities. The league announced that the competition will return in February 2028, marking the first edition of the tournament since 2016.The event is expected to feature the world's top hockey players representing their national teams in a best-on-best tournament.According to reports, the league is going to host the World Cup in Edmonton, Calgary, and the Czech capital, Prague, which will host the 2028 World Cup of Hockey, the NHL announced on Monday. The round-robin will be split between Calgary and Prague, while the semifinals and final are set for Edmonton.Announcing the same on X/Twitter, the NHL stated that Calgary, Edmonton, and Prague will be the hosts for the 2028 World Cup of Hockey! #WCOH”Meanwhile, NHL commissioner Gary Bettman visited Calgary, where a $1.2 billion event centre is slated to open in March 2027. After visiting the site, Bettman said, “I don't think there'll be a nicer building anywhere than that. It looks like it's going to be a spectacular, first-class, state-of-the-art arena. I'm excited about it, and Flames fans will be excited about it when they have a chance to experience it."He further added, “It's going to be great for the City of Calgary to have this type of facility, which, in addition to hosting the Flames, will be able to host concerts and other events that don't currently come to Calgary."It is also reported that the Edmonton portion of the tournament will take place at the Oilers' home rink, Rogers Place, and games in Prague will be hosted at the O2 Arena, one of Europe’s premier hockey venues.
Article Summary: NHL announces World Cup of Hockey 2028, multiple cities to host the tournament. The tournament is scheduled for February 2028 and will feature eight national teams. According to reports, the event last took place in 2016, when Canada captured the title in Toronto.

Article Title: Who is Sudarshan Yellamaraju? India born golfer who learned on YouTube without lessons now chasing PGA glory
Article Text:
Who is Sudarshan Yellamaraju? India born golfer who learned on YouTube without lessons now chasing PGA glory (Image via Getty)

How Sudarshan Yellamaraju learned golf from YouTube videos of Tiger Woods and Rory McIlroy before reaching the PGA Tour



Sudarshan Yellamaraju is not a typical PGA Tour golfer. The 24-year-old rookie learned golf by watching YouTube videos with his father and never took a single professional lesson. Yet on Sunday at the Players Championship at TPC Sawgrass in Ponte Vedra Beach, Florida, he found himself climbing the leaderboard in his very first appearance at the tournament. The Canadian golfer, who was born in India, fired a bogey-free 66 on Saturday and continued pushing forward on Sunday. His strong play gave him an outside chance to compete for the title at one of the PGA Tour’s biggest events.Sudarshan Yellamaraju is playing only his seventh event as a PGA Tour member.But his story has already become one of the most surprising in golf this season. According to reporting from Postmedia, Sudarshan Yellamaraju said he felt confident after his strong round at TPC Sawgrass.“You are not going to get many tougher courses than this,” Sudarshan Yellamaraju told Postmedia. “If I can shoot a round like that and stay in control like I did, that is pretty good. But golf is golf and you never know what might happen tomorrow, so I just have to stay focused.”Sudarshan Yellamaraju’s journey to the PGA Tour started far away from big stadium crowds and famous coaches. He was born in India and moved with his family to Winnipeg, Canada, when he was four years old. A few years later, his father Suresh Yellamaraju took him to a local indoor golf dome. That is where six-year-old Sudarshan first tried hitting golf balls using rental clubs.Those clubs were not ideal. Sudarshan Yellamaraju later said they were heavy men’s clubs with small heads. But the young golfer quickly became curious about the sport. Instead of hiring a coach, Sudarshan Yellamaraju began learning by watching videos. He spent hours studying swings from players like Tiger Woods, Rory McIlroy, and Adam Scott. At home and on the driving range, he would try to copy what he saw.“I would watch videos of Tiger, Rory, Adam Scott, anybody really,” Sudarshan Yellamaraju said. “When you watch live golf you cannot stop it and look again. But on YouTube I could watch things again and again and try to understand what they were doing.”The process was simple but slow. Sudarshan Yellamaraju and his father used trial and error, trying different swings until something worked.“Some things worked and some things did not,” Sudarshan Yellamaraju explained. “We had to figure out what parts of the swing worked for me.”When Sudarshan Yellamaraju was eleven, his family moved to the Toronto area. He kept improving and by the age of sixteen he won the Ontario Amateur Championship. Unlike many top golfers, he did not go to college in the United States. After taking a gap year, Sudarshan Yellamaraju decided to turn professional at nineteen.His professional path slowly moved forward. In 2024 he earned a Korn Ferry Tour card through qualifying school. Then during the 2025 season he won the second event of the year and finished 19th on the Korn Ferry Tour points list, which earned him his first PGA Tour card.Now, in his rookie season, Sudarshan Yellamaraju is already making noise at TPC Sawgrass. During Saturday’s round he made four straight birdies on the front nine, drawing attention from fans watching nearby groups that included Scottie Scheffler and Rory McIlroy. Even now, with access to top coaches on the PGA Tour, Sudarshan Yellamaraju still does not work with one.“Nope,” Sudarshan Yellamaraju said when asked if he had ever taken a lesson. “I am a feel player. If I see the ball going one direction too much, I just try to figure out how to change it.”For a golfer who learned the game by watching videos and experimenting with his father, that approach has taken him all the way to one of golf’s biggest stages.
Article Summary: Sudarshan Yellamaraju learned golf by watching YouTube videos with his father. The 24-year-old rookie never took a single professional lesson. He is playing his first PGA Tour event at the Players Championship at TPC Sawgrass.

Article Title: Lindsey Vonn Comeback: Lindsey Vonn reacts to backlash over her comeback as skiing world praises Marcel Hirscher for similar return
Article Text:
Lindsey Vonn reacts to backlash over her comeback as skiing world praises Marcel Hirscher for similar return (Image Via Getty)

Lindsey Vonn questions comeback criticism as Marcel Hirscher praised while Mikaela Shiffrin points to gender bias

American ski legend Lindsey Vonn has spoken out after new retirement rumors began spreading online. The Olympic champion quickly shut them down and made it clear she is not done yet. The discussion started when a fan account on X suggested that Vonn might soon retire. The comment spread fast, and many people began debating her future again. Vonn responded directly, writing, “Who said I was retiring?” But the conversation did not stop there. Instead, it opened a bigger debate about how women athletes are treated compared to men.Vonn said she believes criticism around her comeback and career decisions shows clear sexism in sport.She pointed to the very different reactions when another skiing star, Marcel Hirscher, returned to competition. According to Vonn, the reactions were completely opposite. When Hirscher announced his comeback, fans and media praised him. When she returned to skiing after surgery, many people questioned her decision and even criticized her personally. The Olympic champion said the contrast shows how female athletes are often judged differently.When Lindsey Vonn officially announced her return to competitive skiing in November 2024, it surprised many fans. Earlier that year she had undergone a partial knee replacement. At 40 years old, some critics questioned whether returning to racing was the right move.A few former athletes even made strong comments about it. Austrian Olympic champion Michaela Dorfmeister reportedly said, “She should see a psychologist. Does she want to kill herself?” Another Austrian skiing legend, Franz Klammer, also criticized the comeback, saying Vonn had “gone completely mad.”However, Vonn pointed out that male athletes returning at an older age often receive praise instead of criticism. She mentioned the comeback of Marcel Hirscher, the eight time overall FIS Alpine Ski World Cup champion.Vonn explained the difference clearly. She said,“No one asked Marcel if his life was fulfilled outside of ski racing, or if he needed to see a psychologist.”Hirscher himself even noticed the difference in reactions. Speaking about the criticism aimed at Vonn, he said:“From my point of view, it is very unfair. I can’t understand those messages because for me it is just inspiring.”The debate around women athletes facing tougher judgment has been discussed before in skiing.Another American star, Mikaela Shiffrin, faced a similar moment during the 2022 Winter Olympics in Beijing. After failing to finish two of her strongest events, TV cameras showed her sitting alone on the course for more than 20 minutes.Some viewers felt the moment was too harsh to broadcast. Others defended the coverage.At the time, NBC executive producer Molly Solomon said:“We have an obligation in that moment to cover the moment. There’s no script when there’s a wipeout on the slopes or a fall in figure skating.”She also admitted that women’s sports still face a double standard.“Here we are in 2022 and we have a double standard in coverage of women’s sports. Women’s sports should be analyzed through the same lens as the men.”For Vonn and Shiffrin, these moments show a bigger issue. Even today, some female athletes still feel they are judged more harshly than men in elite sport.
Article Summary: Lindsey Vonn has spoken out after new retirement rumors began spreading online. The Olympic champion said she believes criticism around her comeback and career decisions shows clear sexism in sport. She pointed to the very different reactions when another skiing star, Marcel Hirscher, returned to competition.

Article Title: Charlie Kirk tragedy resurfaces as trolls mock Utah Valley after Isaac Davis missed dunk ends March Madness dream
Article Text:
Charlie Kirk tragedy resurfaces as trolls mock Utah Valley after Isaac Davis missed dunk ends March Madness dream (Image via Getty)

Dominique Daniels Jr leads Cal Baptist past Utah Valley as Isaac Davis miss sparks online backlash

A tense college basketball game ended in heartbreak for Utah Valley. But what happened online after the final play shocked many fans. Utah Valley lost 63–61 to Cal Baptist in the Western Athletic Conference championship game on Saturday night in Paradise, Nevada. The defeat meant the Wolverines missed an automatic place in the NCAA Tournament, which would have been the first March Madness appearance in school history. In the final seconds, Utah Valley guard Isaac Davis tried to tie the game with a dunk. The attempt missed, and the buzzer sounded moments later.Soon after the highlight was posted online, several people on X began sharing cruel jokes that referenced the 2025 shooting death of political activist Charlie Kirk at Utah Valley University.Many fans quickly pushed back, calling the comments insensitive and disrespectful. Charlie Kirk was fatally shot on September 10, 2025 while speaking with students at Utah Valley University in Orem, Utah. Police later arrested Tyler James Robinson in connection with the case.While the online reaction stirred anger, the game itself was dramatic from start to finish. Cal Baptist guard Dominique Daniels Jr stepped up late and changed the game in the final minutes.Daniels made three three-pointers in the last two minutes and finished the night with 23 points.His biggest shot came with only 14.6 seconds left. Daniels hit a pull-up three-pointer as the shot clock was about to expire, giving Cal Baptist a two-point lead. Utah Valley still had one last chance. Isaac Davis drove toward the basket for a dunk that could have tied the game. But the ball bounced away, and Cal Baptist held on for the 63–61 victory.Soon after the play spread online, some X users posted tasteless comments about Utah Valley. One post read, “Isaac Davis’ missed dunk is the worst thing to happen in the history of Utah Valley.”Another comment said, “I thought nobody missed at Utah Valley University.” A different user wrote, “It’s a shame. Until today Utah Valley was known for good shooting.” Many fans criticized those posts and said joking about a real tragedy crossed a line.On the court, Utah Valley had strong performances despite the loss. Forward Jackson Holcombe scored 18 points, while Isaac Davis added 12. Trevan Leonhardt also played a key role. He grabbed 10 rebounds and handed out eight assists while defending Daniels throughout the game.For Cal Baptist, Bradey Henige scored 11 points and Jonathan Griman added 10 points before fouling out late. The Lancers improved to 25–8 and extended their program record for wins in a Division I season. The team has now won six straight games.Utah Valley also finished with a 25–8 record. The Wolverines had won seven games in a row before the championship loss and have captured two straight regular-season conference titles. Even with the defeat, Utah Valley will still play in the National Invitation Tournament. Both programs will also begin a new chapter next season when they move to the Big West Conference.
Article Summary: Utah Valley lost 63–61 to Cal Baptist in the Western Athletic Conference championship game on Saturday night in Paradise, Nevada. The defeat meant the Wolverines missed an automatic place in the NCAA Tournament. In the final seconds, Utah Valley guard Isaac Davis tried to tie the game with a dunk. The attempt missed, and the buzzer sounded moments later.

Article Title: How Azhar Mahmood played IPL despite Pakistan player ban since 2009
Article Text:
Azhar Mahmood

Jay Shah calls 2019 to 2026 as Indian cricket's golden period

Pakistan players in IPL

Azhar Mahmood: Last Pakistan player in IPL

For more than a decade, Pakistan players have remained absent from the Indian Premier League (IPL). The last cricketer from Pakistan to feature in the tournament was allrounder Azhar Mahmood, who played in the league between 2012 and 2015, although he did so after entering the auction as an English player. His appearance came years after the participation of Pakistan cricketers ended in the IPL following the 2008 Mumbai terror attack.The discussion around Pakistan players and franchise leagues returned recently after mystery spinner Abrar Ahmed was picked by Sunrisers Leeds during the player auction of The Hundred. The franchise, owned by the Chennai-based Sun Group, paid GBP 190000 (Rs 2.34 crore approximately) after a bidding contest with Trent Rockets.With that move, Ahmed became the first Pakistani player to be signed by an Indian-owned franchise in the tournament.His signing also ended speculation that Indian-owned teams in The Hundred would avoid Pakistan players, similar to what has happened in the IPL since 2009 due to strained diplomatic relations between India and Pakistan.The first season of the IPL in 2008 included several Pakistani cricketers, including Shoaib Akhtar, Shahid Afridi, and Sohail Tanvir.The tournament saw players from both countries share dressing rooms and compete in the new T20 league.Shahid Afridi played for Deccan Chargers, while fast bowler Shoaib Akhtar represented Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR). Misbah-ul-Haq was part of the squad of Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB).Among them, it was fast bowler Sohail Tanvir who left the biggest mark. Playing for Rajasthan Royals (RR), who won the league in 2008, Tanvir finished the season with 22 wickets and claimed the Purple Cap for the highest number of wickets in the tournament. His best performance came against Chennai Super Kings (CSK) when he returned figures of 6/14.However, the situation changed after the 2008 Mumbai terror attacks. Following the attacks, political ties between India and Pakistan were suspended. Later, Pakistan also barred its players from travelling to India for the IPL, citing security concerns.At the time, several franchises expressed disappointment over the absence of Pakistani players from the league.Joy Bhattacharya, who was the then team leader of the KKR, said the team would miss the players from Pakistan. Akhtar, Umar Gul, Salman Butt and Mohammad Hafeez had all represented the franchise in 2008."We were like a family. Shoaib and Umar are not only good cricketers but popular among their team-mates as well at the Knight Riders. We will surely miss them a lot," he had said.Similar views were shared by Amrit Mathur, who was then the chief executive of Delhi Daredevils."We are going to miss the Pakistani players because they were key members of our team," Mathur had said.What was initially expected to be a temporary break turned into a long absence. Over time, obtaining No-Objection Certificates from the Pakistan Cricket Board became difficult. At the same time, IPL franchises were hesitant to bid for players who might not receive visas to travel to India.While Pakistan players have largely stayed away from the IPL since 2009, Azhar Mahmood did appear in the league for three seasons between 2012 and 2015.Mahmood played for Punjab Kings (then Kings XI Punjab) in the 2012 and 2013 seasons and later represented KKR in the 2015 season.However, Mahmood did not enter the league as a Pakistani player. After moving to the United Kingdom and obtaining a British passport, he registered for the IPL auction as an English player.A right-arm fast-bowling allrounder, Mahmood played 23 IPL matches across three seasons. He took 29 wickets while conceding runs at an economy rate of 7.82. With the bat, he scored 388 runs at a strike rate of 128.05.Mahmood remains the last Pakistan-born cricketer to feature in the IPL, even though he did so as a British citizen.In 2010, there was a brief moment when Pakistan players returned to the IPL auction list. Several leading players from the country were included in the auction pool. However, no franchise placed a bid on any of them. The episode later came to be described as a “silent snub” and it effectively confirmed that the IPL had moved on from signing Pakistan cricketers.Since then, the absence of Pakistan players from the IPL has continued. Mahmood’s appearance in the tournament remains the final instance of a Pakistan-born cricketer taking the field in the league, marking the end of a short chapter that began in the first season of the IPL in 2008.
Article Summary: Pakistan players have been absent from the Indian Premier League since 2009. The last Pakistan-born cricketer to feature in the IPL was Azhar Mahmood. Mahmood played in the league for three seasons between 2012 and 2015. He did so after entering the auction as an English player.

Article Title: IPL Tickets Online: How to book IPL 2026 tickets online and offline, full IPL match schedule and other details
Article Text:
IPL 2026

IPL 2026 opening match



IPL 2026: Phase 1 schedule and double headers



Bengaluru

Mumbai

Guwahati

New Chandigarh

Lucknow

Kolkata

Chennai

Delhi

Ahmedabad

Hyderabad

The phase will feature four double-headers, with afternoon matches starting at 3:30 PM IST and evening matches beginning at 7:30 PM IST. The first double-header of IPL 2026 takes place on April 4, when Delhi Capitals host Mumbai Indians at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in Delhi in the afternoon It will be followed by Gujarat Titans vs Rajasthan Royals at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad in the evening.

IPL 2026 home venues: Who is playing where



Royal Challengers Bengaluru will play five home matches in Bengaluru and two in Raipur.

Punjab Kings (PBKS) will host four matches at the PCA Stadium in New Chandigarh and three in Dharamshala.

Rajasthan Royals will begin their home campaign in Guwahati, where they will host Chennai Super Kings and Mumbai Indians. They will play four matches in Jaipur.

How to book IPL 2026 tickets online



Visit BookMyShow (bookmyshow.com), the District app (by Zomato), or your team's official website.

Search for the IPL 2026 match you want to attend using the date or team name.

Select your preferred seating category.

Choose your specific seats using the interactive stadium map.

Add the tickets to your cart and proceed to checkout.

Complete payment using a credit card, debit card, UPI, net banking, or digital wallet.

The ticket will be sent to your registered email address or mobile number.

How to buy IPL 2026 tickets offline



Visit the nearest authorised ticket counter at the stadium or a designated retail outlet. Locations are typically announced on the team’s official website and social media closer to the match.

Check availability for your preferred match and seating category at the counter.

Carry a valid photo ID — an Aadhaar card, PAN card, or passport will be accepted.

Choose your seats and confirm your selection with the ticketing staff.

Make your payment — cash, card, and digital wallets are generally accepted at most venues.

Collect your ticket on the spot after the transaction is complete.

The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has announced the schedule for the first phase of the TATA Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026, and cricket fans across the country are already scrambling for tickets. The tournament begins on March 28, and the first phase runs through to April 12. The board has released the list of first 20 matches and a full schedule for the remainder of the tournament will be released once election dates are confirmed for three states that are set to hold State Assembly elections during this period.The 19th edition of IPL will begin with a blockbuster clash at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru, where defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) will take on Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) on March 28.Mumbai Indians face Kolkata Knight Riders at the iconic Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai the next day.A total of 20 matches will be played across 10 venues during the first phase:Several teams are splitting their home games across multiple cities this season:The BCCI has not yet officially reveled any information about the ticket sales but the process is expected to follow the same pattern as previous years.BookMyShow has already opened pre-sale registration for tickets to Mumbai Indians and Gujarat Titans home matches on its website and mobile app. Meanwhile, Zomato's District app has also prompted tickets via a dedicated page for IPL 2026 tickets. Official team websites are also expected to be ticketing channels once the booking window opens.Once sales go live, here is how to purchase your tickets:

Royal Challengers Bengaluru will play five home matches in Bengaluru and two in Raipur. Rajasthan Royals will begin their home campaign in Guwahati, where they will host Chennai Super Kings and Mumbai Indians.

Article Title: Purdue Boilermakers center Oscar Cluff creates viral moment after mixing up Queens University location before March Madness matchup
Article Text:
Purdue Boilermakers center Oscar Cluff creates viral moment after mixing up Queens University location before March Madness matchup (Image via Getty)

Purdue Boilermakers center Oscar Cluff laughs off viral Queens University location mistake before NCAA Tournament matchup

Oscar Cluff became an unexpected talking point just as the NCAA March Madness tournament bracket was revealed. The Purdue Boilermakers center went viral online after making a simple but embarrassing mistake while speaking about his team’s next opponent. Purdue had just secured the Big Ten Championship and earned the No. 2 seed in the West Region of the NCAA Tournament. That win set up a first-round matchup against the Atlantic Sun Conference champions, the Queens University Royals.Soon after the bracket announcement, WJW reporter Angela Moryan asked Oscar Cluff what he knew about Queens University. His answer quickly spread across social media.“It’s in New York somewhere,” Cluff said.The moment became awkward almost instantly. Queens University is not in New York. The school is located in Charlotte, North Carolina, which is about 540 miles away from New York City.As soon as Cluff realized the mistake, the situation turned into a lighthearted moment among teammates. Cameras caught players laughing while Cluff walked away looking embarrassed. Despite the slip, Purdue still heads into the matchup as one of the strongest teams in the tournament.Oscar Cluff’s comment happened during a short courtside interview with reporter Angela Moryan shortly after Purdue’s conference title win.When asked about the upcoming opponent, Cluff sounded excited and focused on the game itself.“Can’t wait, let’s get it going,” Cluff said.Angela Moryan then followed up with another question, asking what he already knew about Queens University. That is when the viral moment happened.“It’s in New York somewhere,” Cluff replied.Teammates standing nearby quickly reacted. Several players laughed while Cluff realized the mistake and walked away from the camera looking a bit red-faced.Trying to move past the moment, Cluff added another quick response.“Just ready to play, ready to play whoever,” he said.Queens University actually sits in Charlotte, North Carolina. The school’s name comes from the city’s history. Charlotte itself was named after Queen Charlotte, the wife of British King George III. Because of that royal connection, Charlotte is often called “The Queen City.”The name Queens University also honors a former school called Queen’s College that was founded there in 1771 during the time of British rule.While Cluff’s mix-up created laughs online, the game itself looks very one-sided on paper. According to ESPN’s matchup predictor, Purdue has a 97.8 percent chance of winning against Queens University in the first round of the NCAA Tournament.Even so, March Madness is famous for surprising results. Lower-seed teams have shocked favorites many times before, which is why fans will still watch closely when Purdue and Queens finally meet on the court.
Article Summary: Purdue Boilermakers center Oscar Cluff made a simple but embarrassing mistake while speaking about his team's next opponent. Queens University is not in New York. The school is located in Charlotte, North Carolina, which is about 540 miles away from New York City.

Article Title: "She stayed for their family": Dak Prescott’s ex fiancée Sarah Jane Ramos reportedly went through a lot of pain during their three year relationship
Article Text:
Dak Prescott’s ex fiancée Sarah Jane Ramos reportedly went through a lot of pain during their three year relationship.(Image via Getty Images)

Dak Prescott’s ex fiancee Sarah Jane Ramos might have gone through a lot in their relationship



Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos informed their guests together



Dak Prescott, the Dallas Cowboys star player, and his ex fiancee, Sarah Jane Ramos, canceled their Lake Como wedding, sparking panic among fans.A few hours ago, as per Page Six, a source has revealed that Dak Prescott reportedly cheated on her multiple times throughout their three year old relationship.Sources have added that throughout all the mess, Sarah Jane Ramos has stayed with the Dallas Cowboys’ star player, Dak Prescott, “for the sake of their family”.However, neither Dak Prescott nor his ex fiancee Sarah Jane Ramos has responded to the cheating allegations yet as they seek privacy from all the mess.Interestingly, over the last three years that Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos were together, there were no cheating rumors as the high profile couple seemed pretty happy and content with each other and their two young daughters, Margaret and Aurora.For the unversed, though their high profile relationship seemed perfect from outside, things came crashing down at their joint bachelor and bachelorette parties in the Bahamas.As per reports, Dak Prescott was allegedly caught texting other women and ex flings through a “burner” social account by his ex fiancee, Sarah Jane Ramos.This resulted in a huge argument between the two and it has been reported that it was Sarah Jane Ramos was the one who canceled their lavish Lake Como wedding.As per TMZ Sports, Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos sent a joint mail to their 250 guests who were supposed to join their Italian wedding.The mail read as, "It is with heavy hearts that we privately inform our attendees that we have made the very difficult decision to cancel our wedding.”The mail added, “We deeply apologize for any inconvenience this may cause you and we welcome your prayers."The email was officially signed as, "Sarah Jane Ramos and Dak Prescott."At this point in time, both Dak Prescott and Sarah Jane Ramos have seeked privacy from all the mess in their lives as they focus on the upbringing and well being of their two young daughters.
Article Summary: Dak Prescott and his ex fiancee, Sarah Jane Ramos, canceled their Lake Como wedding, sparking panic among fans. A source has revealed that Dak Prescott reportedly cheated on her multiple times throughout their three year old relationship. Neither Dak Prescott nor his ex-fiancée has responded to the cheating allegations yet.
